content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
April 5, 1996

Norman Rubin
3332 Tice Creek Drive, No. 4
Walnut Creek, CA 94595

RE: Your Request For Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-96-26

Dear Mr. Rubin:

This is in response to your request for advice on behalf of
the Rossmoor Democrats' Club (the "Club") regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ Since your
inquiry is general in nature, we are treating your letter as a
request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

QUESTION

You have asked whether the Club qualified as a recipient
committee as a result of receiving $1,100.19 as its share of a
fundraiser hosted by the Diablo Valley Democratic Club.

CONCLUSION

Because the Club has not made or received contributions or
made expenditures totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar year to
support or oppose California state and/or local candidates or
measures, it did not qualify as a committee and therefore has no
filing obligation under the Act.

_________________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

Norman Rubin
Our File No. I-96-26
Page 2


FACTS

The Rossmoor Democrats' Club received $1,100.19 as its share
of a fundraiser hosted by the Diablo Valley Democratic Club. It
is the intention of the Rossmoor Democrats' Club to support
federal candidates as well as California state and local
candidates. It is anticipated that the majority of the $1,100.19
will be contributed to federal candidates. The Club filed a
Statement of Organization (Form 410) executed on January 10, 1996,
with the Secretary of State's office indicating that it had not
yet qualified as a recipient committee.


ANALYSIS

The Act requires the filing of periodic campaign disclosure
reports by "committees." Section 82013 defines a "committee" as:

...any person or combination of persons who
directly or indirectly does any of the following:

(a) Receives contributions totaling one thousand
dollars ($1,000) or more in a calendar year;

(b) Makes independent expenditures totaling one
thousand ($1,000) or more in a calendar year; or

(c) Makes contributions totaling ten thousand dollars
($10,000) or more in a calendar year to or at the behest of
candidates or committees.

Section 82015 and Regulation 18215 define "contribution" as a
payment made for political purposes. A payment is made for
political purposes if it is made "for the purpose of influencing
or attempting to influence the action of the voters for or against
the nomination or election of a candidate or candidates, or the
qualification or passage of any measure..." or is received by or

Norman Rubin
Our File No. I-96-26
Page 3


made at the behest of a candidate or a committee. The term
"contribution" includes, but is not limited to:

     Any payment made to a person or organization other
than a candidate or committee, when, at the time of
making the payment, the donor knows or has reason to
know that the payment, or funds with which the payment
will be commingled, will be used to make contributions.
If the donor knows or has reason to know that only part
of the payment will be used to make contributions or
expenditures, the payment shall be apportioned on a
reasonable basis in order to determine the amount of the
contribution.

     There shall be a presumption that the donor does
not have reason to know that all or part of the payment
will be used for expenditures or contributions, unless
the person or organization has made expenditures or
contributions of at least one thousand dollars ($1,000)
in the aggregate during the calendar year in which the
payment occurs, or any of the immediately preceding
four calendar years.

                              (Regulation 18215
                              Emphasis added.)

The term "independent expenditure" means:

     ...an expenditure made by any person in connection
with a communication which expressly advocates the election
or defeat of a clearly identified candidate or the
qualification, passage or defeat of a clearly identified
measure, or taken as a whole and in context, unambiguously
urges a particular result in an election but which is
not made to or at the behest of the affected candidate or
committee.

                              (Section 82031.)

The term "made to or at the behest of" means:

     ...made under the control or at the direction of, in
cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with
at the request or suggestion of, or with the express, prior
consent of the affected candidate/committee.

                              (Regulation 18225.7.)

Norman Rubin
Our File No. I-96-26
Page 4


Because the Act's jurisdiction is limited to California state
and local elections, an organization like the Rossmoor Democrats'
Club only becomes a "committee" if it receives contributions or
makes independent expenditures totaling $1,000 or more in a
calendar year to support or oppose California state and/or local
candidates and measures. Based on your assertion that the
majority of the $1,100.19 collected at this fundraiser will be
used to support federal candidates, the Club will not qualify as a
committee under the Act. However, if the Club makes contributions
or expenditures totaling $1,000 or more during 1996 to support
state and/or local candidates or committees, it will be required
to register and begin filing campaign reports at that time.

During our telephone conversation of February 6, 1996, I
suggested that you file a Form 415, Statement of Termination, with
the Secretary of State's office and a copy with your local filing
officer. I also suggested you enclose a cover letter with the
Form 415 explaining the Club never qualified as a committee.
Also, during our telephone conversation, you indicated that it is
still the club's intention to make contributions to federal
candidates and that contributions to California state and local
candidates would not total $1,000 or more during the calendar
year. You may wish to contact the Federal Elections Commission at
(800) 424-9530 regarding possible federal campaign reporting
requirements.

If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact
me at 916/322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Mary Ann Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant
